guidelines.authors: Lappin MR, Blondeau J, Boothe D, et al.
guidelines.title: Antimicrobial use Guidelines for Treatment of Respiratory Tract Disease in Dogs and Cats

guidelines.authors: Dear JD
guidelines.title: Bacterial Pneumonia in Dogs and Cats: An Update
guidelines.journal: Vet Clin North Am Small Anim Pract

fluid_bolus.rationale: Restore perfusion, careful to avoid fluid overload worsening pulmonary edema

guidelines.authors: Segev G, Cortellini S, Foster JD, et al.
guidelines.title: IRIS Best Practice Consensus Guidelines for AKI in Cats and Dogs

guidelines.authors: Cowgill LD, Elliott DA, Grauer GF
guidelines.title: IRIS Staging of CKD and AKI: Updated 2023 Guidelines

ph_low.mechanism: Impaired H+ excretion, reduced HCO3- reabsorption

t_wave_tall.mechanism: Accelerated repolarization from elevated extracellular K+

qrs_wide.mechanism: Slowed ventricular conduction; further widening → sine wave at K+ >8.0

SDMA_high.mechanism: SDMA rises earlier than creatinine (25-50% GFR loss vs 75% for Cr); less affected by muscle mass

urine_output_low.mechanism: Nephron damage -> impaired concentrating ability or tubular obstruction

USG_low.mechanism: Loss of >67% nephron function -> inability to concentrate urine

stage_1.clinical: Non-azotemic AKI: oliguria/anuria, urine sediment abnormalities, or imaging evidence

fluid_bolus.rationale: Balance crystalloids (LRS or Normosol) to restore renal perfusion; avoid fluid overload in oliguric patients

furosemide.rationale: Loop diuretic to promote urine output; given after initial fluid resuscitation

antiemetic.rationale: Control nausea/vomiting from uremia and gastric irritation (maropitant or ondansetron)

phosphate_binder.rationale: Bind enteric phosphate when hyperphosphatemia is severe (e.g., aluminum hydroxide)

peritoneal_dialysis.rationale: Indicated for refractory oliguria/anuria, severe hyperkalemia, or fluid overload unresponsive to diuretics

guidelines.authors: Fuentes VL, Abbott J, Chetboul V, et al.
guidelines.title: ACVIM Consensus Statement: Guidelines for DCM in Dogs

guidelines.authors: Dukes-McEwan J, Borgarelli M, Tidholm A, et al.
guidelines.title: Proposed Guidelines for the Diagnosis of Canine Idiopathic Dilated Cardiomyopathy

hr_high.mechanism: Reduced stroke volume → compensatory HR increase

LA_AO_high.mechanism: Left atrial dilation from mitral regurgitation

EPSS.mechanism: Increased EPSS reflects reduced LV ejection

guidelines.authors: Gray SL, Lee JA, Hovda LR, Brutlag AG
guidelines.title: Potential zinc phosphide rodenticide toxicosis in dogs: 362 cases

guidelines.authors: Osweiler GD, Hovda LR, Brutlag AG, Lee JA
guidelines.title: Blackwell's Five-Minute Veterinary Consult Clinical Companion: Small Animal Toxicology

hr_high.mechanism: Compensatory response to hypotension + direct cardiotoxicity

ph_low.mechanism: High anion gap metabolic acidosis from impaired oxidative phosphorylation

garlic_breath.mechanism: Phosphine gas release from gastric acid reaction with zinc phosphide

mechanism.effect: Disrupts oxidative phosphorylation → cellular anoxia + ROS

phosphine_toxicokinetics.mechanism_detail: Phosphine gas released in stomach acid -> mitochondrial complex IV inhibition -> cellular hypoxia

organ_damage_timeline.GI: 0-2h: vomiting, abdominal pain, garlic odor on breath

guidelines.authors: Glickman LT, Glickman NW, Perez CM, et al.
guidelines.title: Analysis of risk factors for GDV in dogs: 1934 cases

guidelines.authors: Mooney E, Raw C, Hughes D
guidelines.title: Plasma lactate as prognostic biomarker in GDV

arrhythmia.mechanism: Myocardial ischemia, reperfusion injury; onset 12-36h post-surgery

guidelines.authors: Garden OA, Kidd L, Mexas AM, et al.
guidelines.title: ACVIM Consensus: Diagnosis of IMHA in Dogs and Cats

guidelines.authors: Swann JW, Garden OA, Fellman CL, et al.
guidelines.title: ACVIM Consensus: Treatment of IMHA in Dogs

hr_high.mechanism: Compensatory for anemia → reduced O2 carrying capacity

bilirubin_high.mechanism: Hemoglobin → heme → bilirubin from RBC destruction

guidelines.authors: McLoughlin MA
guidelines.title: Surgical emergencies of the urinary tract
guidelines.journal: Vet Clin North Am Small Anim Pract

guidelines.authors: Langston VC, Reineks R
guidelines.title: Urolithiasis
guidelines.journal: Nephrology and Urology of Small Animals

potassium_high.mechanism: Impaired renal K+ excretion from obstruction

t_wave_tall.mechanism: Accelerated repolarization — earliest ECG sign of hyperkalemia

qrs_wide.mechanism: Slowed ventricular conduction; sine wave pattern at K+ >8.0

guidelines.authors: Gal A, Odunayo A
guidelines.title: DKA and HHS in companion animals
guidelines.journal: Vet Clin North Am Small Anim Pract

guidelines.authors: Monnet E, et al.
guidelines.title: Accuracy of capillary BHB for detection of canine DKA

rr_high.mechanism: Respiratory compensation for metabolic acidosis: pCO2 ↓0.7 per 1 mEq/L HCO3- ↓

ketone_high.mechanism: Hepatic ketogenesis from fatty acid oxidation

potassium_low.mechanism: Osmotic diuresis loss + insulin-driven intracellular shift

guidelines.authors: Shaw SP, Rush JE
guidelines.title: Canine pericardial effusion: diagnosis, treatment, and prognosis

guidelines.authors: DeFrancesco TC
guidelines.title: Management of cardiac emergencies in small animals
guidelines.journal: Vet Clin North Am Small Anim Pract

map_low.mechanism: Cardiac tamponade → impaired filling → reduced CO

cvp_high.mechanism: Impaired right heart filling → venous congestion

SpO2_low.mechanism: Reduced cardiac output → poor tissue perfusion

muffled_heart_sounds.mechanism: Fluid buffer between heart and chest wall

jugular_distension.mechanism: Elevated CVP from impaired venous return

pericardiocentesis_indication.principle: Pericardiocentesis is indicated when echocardiographic evidence of cardiac tamponade physiology is present — NOT based on a specific fluid volume threshold
echo_signs: RA diastolic collapse (>1/3 cardiac cycle duration)
RV diastolic collapse (right ventricular diastolic compression)
IVC plethora (dilated IVC with <50% collapse on inspiration)
Respirophasic variation in transvalvular flows (>25% variation in mitral inflow)
pericardiocentesis_indication.source: Shaw 2007; DeFrancesco 2013; ACVIM Cardiac Disease Consensus 2022
pericardiocentesis_indication.note: Even small volumes (<50 mL) can cause life-threatening tamponade if accumulated rapidly; slow accumulation may allow >200 mL without clinical signs

guidelines.authors: Taylor FB Jr, Toh CH, Hoots WK, Wada H, Levi M
guidelines.title: ISTH scoring system for DIC

guidelines.authors: Wiinberg B, Jensen AL, Johansson PI, et al.
guidelines.title: Canine DIC scoring model

hemorrhage.mechanism: Consumption of clotting factors + secondary fibrinolysis

guidelines.authors: Kelley D, Lester C, Shaw S, de Laforcade A, Webster CRL
guidelines.title: Thromboelastographic Evaluation of Dogs with Acute Liver Disease

guidelines.authors: Gow AG
guidelines.title: Hepatic Encephalopathy
guidelines.journal: Vet Clin North Am Small Anim Pract

pt_prolonged.mechanism: Decreased synthesis of vitamin K-dependent factors (II, VII, IX, X)

fibrinogen_low.mechanism: Decreased hepatic synthesis; distinguishes from DIC (bimodal) and rodenticide (increased)

albumin_low.mechanism: Decreased hepatic synthesis; most consistent finding in cirrhosis (96%)

ammonia_high.mechanism: Impaired urea cycle → hyperammonemia → astrocyte swelling → HE

alt_high.mechanism: Hepatocyte leakage; 40-65% sensitivity alone, 90% combined with ALP

guidelines.authors: Schick AR, Grimes JA
guidelines.title: Double two-thirds rule for diagnosing HSA: systematic review

guidelines.authors: Hammond TN, Pesillo-Crosby SA
guidelines.title: Prevalence of HSA in anemic dogs with splenic mass

hct_low.mechanism: Hemorrhage; HSA significantly lower than benign

guidelines.authors: Peterson ME
guidelines.title: Hyperthyroidism in cats: what's causing this epidemic of thyroid disease?

guidelines.authors: Carney HC, Ward CR, Bailey SJ, et al.
guidelines.title: 2016 AAFP Guidelines for the Management of Feline Hyperthyroidism

T3_high.mechanism: Autonomous thyroid hormone secretion from adenomatous tissue

temp_high.mechanism: T3 increases basal metabolic rate → hyperthermia

map_high.mechanism: T3 increases cardiac output and decreases SVR

guidelines.authors: Melian C, Peterson ME
guidelines.title: Diagnosis and treatment of naturally occurring hypoadrenocorticism in 42 dogs

guidelines.authors: Klein SC, Peterson ME
guidelines.title: Canine hypoadrenocorticism: part I and II

map_low.mechanism: Cortisol deficiency → loss of vascular tone + hypovolemia

potassium_high.mechanism: Aldosterone deficiency → impaired renal K+ excretion

na_k_ratio.mechanism: Simultaneous hyponatremia + hyperkalemia from mineralocorticoid deficiency

guidelines.authors: Singer M, Deutschman CS, Seymour CW, et al.
guidelines.title: The Third International Consensus Definitions for Sepsis and Septic Shock (Sepsis-3)

guidelines.authors: Sharp CR, Kerl ME
guidelines.title: Sepsis in dogs and cats: a review

map_low.mechanism: Vasodilation from cytokine storm → distributive shock

guidelines.authors: De Risio L, Thomas WB, Sharp NJ
guidelines.title: Degenerative lumbosacral stenosis
guidelines.journal: Vet Clin North Am Small Anim Pract

guidelines.authors: Levine JM, Levine GJ, Johnson SI, et al.
guidelines.title: Evaluation of the success of medical management for presumptive thoracolumbar intervertebral disc herniation in dogs

guidelines.authors: Jeffery ND, Levine JM, Olby NJ, Stein VM
guidelines.title: Intervertebral Disk Degeneration in Dogs: Consequences, Rehabilitation, and Clinical Trials

pain_mobility.mechanism: Disc extrusion/protrusion → spinal cord compression + meningeal inflammation

weakness.mechanism: Spinal cord compression → upper motor neuron deficits

deep_pain_absent.threshold: No response to strong toe pinch
deep_pain_absent.mechanism: Complete spinal cord compression -> loss of all sensory pathways (prognostic indicator)

grade_3.approach: Surgery recommended if no improvement in 7-14 days

guidelines.authors: Tipold A
guidelines.title: Diagnosis of inflammatory and infectious diseases of the central nervous system in dogs
guidelines.journal: Vet Clin North Am Small Anim Pract

guidelines.authors: Griffin JF, Levine JM, Levine GJ, et al.
guidelines.title: Meningoencephalitis in dogs: a retrospective study

guidelines.authors: Tipold A, Stein VM
guidelines.title: Inflammatory Diseases of the Meninges
guidelines.journal: Vet Clin North Am Small Anim Pract

csf_wbc_high.mechanism: Blood-brain barrier disruption -> leukocyte migration into CSF

csf_protein_high.mechanism: BBB permeability increase -> serum protein leakage

fever.mechanism: CNS inflammation -> prostaglandin-mediated hypothalamic set-point elevation

mri_findings.parenchymal_lesions: T2/FLAIR hyperintensity in brain/spinal cord parenchyma
mri_findings.hydrocephalus: Internal hydrocephalus from CSF outflow obstruction

guidelines.authors: International Renal Interest Society (IRIS)
guidelines.title: IRIS Staging of CKD (Modified 2024)

guidelines.authors: Chalhoub S, Langston CE, Farrelly J
guidelines.title: The use of darbepoetin to stimulate erythropoiesis in anemia of CKD in cats

guidelines.authors: Chalhoub S, Langston CE, Eatroff A
guidelines.title: Anemia of Renal Disease: What Is It, What to Do and What's New

hct_low.mechanism: Decreased EPO production from damaged nephrons

cr_high.mechanism: Nephron loss → decreased GFR → creatinine retention

potassium_high.mechanism: Decreased renal K+ excretion in advanced CKD

ph_low.mechanism: Decreased renal HCO3- reabsorption → metabolic acidosis

map_high.mechanism: Sodium retention + RAAS activation → renal hypertension

reticulocyte_low.mechanism: Decreased EPO production -> inadequate bone marrow response

transferrin_saturation_low.mechanism: Functional iron deficiency from chronic inflammation + GI blood loss

darbepoetin.dose: 0.5 ug/kg SC weekly until HCT 30-35%, then every 2-4 weeks

EPO_human_recombinant.risk: 20-30% develop anti-EPO antibodies -> pure red cell aplasia

guidelines.authors: Webster CRL, Center SA
guidelines.title: Hepatic lipidosis: update on mechanisms and treatment

guidelines.authors: Toulza O, Center SA, Brooks MB, et al.
guidelines.title: Evaluation of protein C and antithrombin activities in dogs with hepatic disease

guidelines.authors: Rothuizen J, Bunch SE, Charles JA, et al.
guidelines.title: WSAVA Standards for Clinical and Histological Diagnosis of Canine and Feline Liver Diseases

hct_low.mechanism: Decreased EPO, chronic GI blood loss, hemolysis, bone marrow suppression

hr_high.mechanism: Compensatory for anemia → reduced oxygen-carrying capacity

albumin_low_severe.mechanism: Severe hepatic synthetic failure -> hypoalbuminemia -> ascites/edema

hepatic_encephalopathy_grading.grade_1: Mild behavioral changes: confusion, staring, head pressing

coagulopathy_present.survival: Guarded to poor; indicates synthetic failure
